Dexamethasone 4mg once a day oral for 3 days (take home)

Cardiac function must be assessed prior to starting trastuzumab (LVEF must be ≥50%) and twelve weekly thereafter
Trastuzumab dose reductions are NOT permitted under any circumstances
Trastuzumab associated with cardiotoxicity: suspend if LVEF drops ≥10 ejection points from baseline to below 50%; stop if confirmed on repeat assessment
Trastuzumab is associated with hypersensitivity/infusion reactions: observe for 6 hours after first infusion and 2 hours after subsequent infusions
Cisplatin is an exfoliant; fluorouracil is an inflammitant; trastuzumab is neutral — follow extravasation protocols
Patients should drink at least 3 litres of fluid in the 24 hours after cisplatin administration
Fluorouracil is contra-indicated when bilirubin >85 umol/L or AST >180 umol/L
Central venous access and ambulatory infusion pump required
Cisplatin: mandatory IV hydration required — pre-hydration and post-hydration per local cisplatin hydration protocol. Monitor urine output, U&Es, and Mg²⁺ before and after each cycle.

drugs.notes: Dose calculated based on trifluridine component. Twice daily dosing. Maximum dose 80mg trifluridine per dose (160mg/day). Minimum dose 20mg/m² trifluridine. Doses rounded to nearest 5mg (up if halfway). Maximum 3 dose reductions permitted at 5mg/m² increments: 35→30→25→20mg/m². Dose escalation not permitted after reduction. Tablets available as 15mg/6.14mg and 20mg/8.19mg (trifluridine/tipiracil hydrochloride).

Hold until platelets recover to ≥75x10⁹/L before starting next cycle

Not recommended in moderate or severe hepatic impairment
Not recommended in severe renal impairment or end-stage renal disease

Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral (take home)
Ondansetron 8mg twice a day oral for 3 days starting evening of chemotherapy day (take home)
special_warnings: DPD testing required before initiation of fluorouracil; patients with complete or partial DPD deficiency are at increased risk of severe and fatal toxicity
Perioperative regimen: 4 cycles pre-operatively then surgery, then further 4 cycles post-operatively (8 cycles total set in ARIA)
Dexamethasone pre-medication mandatory to prevent docetaxel-induced fluid retention and hypersensitivity reactions
Glucose 5% flush required before and after oxaliplatin; oxaliplatin must only be mixed with glucose 5%
Oxaliplatin cumulative peripheral neuropathy: adjust infusion duration for acute laryngopharyngeal dysaesthesia
Rare reports of oxaliplatin-associated acute interstitial lung disease/lung fibrosis: stop treatment and investigate any unexplained respiratory symptoms
Docetaxel hypersensitivity may occur with first or second infusion; discontinue immediately for severe reactions (hypotension, bronchospasm, generalised erythema)
Central venous access and ambulatory infusion pump required
Fluorouracil is contra-indicated when bilirubin >85 umol/L or AST >180 units
Folinic acid may be replaced with calcium levofolinate 175mg IV in 250ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes

In 500ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes. Glucose 5% flush required before and after. Cumulative peripheral neuropathy.
1400mg/m² total dose per infusion; continuous ambulatory pump over 7 days per dose at days 1, 8, and 15. Central venous access required.

special_warnings: DPD testing required before initiation of fluorouracil; patients with complete or partial DPD deficiency are at increased risk of severe and fatal toxicity
Epirubicin cumulative lifetime limit approximately 900 mg/m²; cardiac failure is a known adverse effect
Baseline LVEF must be measured before starting therapy, particularly in patients with cardiac history or elderly patients
Glucose 5% flush required before and after oxaliplatin; oxaliplatin must only be mixed with glucose 5%
Oxaliplatin cumulative peripheral neuropathy; rare reports of acute interstitial lung disease/lung fibrosis — stop and investigate unexplained respiratory symptoms

drugs.notes: In 500ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes. Glucose 5% flush required before and after. Dose reduction target: 60mg/m². Cumulative peripheral neuropathy; increase infusion to 6 hours for acute laryngopharyngeal dysaesthesia. Contra-indicated if CrCl <20 ml/min.
In 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 90 minutes. Dose reduction target: 150mg/m². Associated with acute cholinergic syndrome; atropine 0.25mg SC required prior to infusion.

In 250ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes. May be replaced with calcium levofolinate 175mg IV in 250ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes. No dose adjustment for haematological counts; omit if fluorouracil is omitted.
IV bolus over 10 minutes (400mg/m²). Given after folinic acid. Omit bolus dose for haematological or diarrhoea toxicity as per dose modification tables.
Continuous intravenous infusion over 46 hours via ambulatory pump. Dose reduction target: 75% of original infusion dose. Central venous access required.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or intravenous 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Atropine sulphate 0.25mg (250mcg) subcutaneous immediately prior to irinotecan for prevention of acute cholinergic syndrome
Dexamethasone 4mg oral twice a day for 3 days (take home)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (take home)
Ondansetron 8mg oral twice a day for 3 days (take home)
Loperamide 2mg every 2 hours once first liquid stool appears; continue until 12 hours after last liquid stool (max 48 hours use)
Consider oral ciprofloxacin 500mg twice daily if diarrhoea continues for more than 24 hours (review patient first)
special_warnings: DPD testing required before initiation of fluorouracil; patients with complete or partial DPD deficiency are at increased risk of severe and fatal toxicity
Irinotecan causes acute cholinergic syndrome: atropine 0.25mg SC must be given immediately prior to each irinotecan infusion and when required
Irinotecan causes delayed diarrhoea (may be severe): aggressive loperamide management required; diarrhoea must have completely resolved before re-treatment
Glucose 5% flush required before and after oxaliplatin; oxaliplatin contra-indicated if CrCl <20ml/min
Oxaliplatin cumulative peripheral neuropathy; for acute laryngopharyngeal dysaesthesia increase infusion time to 6 hours
Rare reports of oxaliplatin-associated acute interstitial lung disease/lung fibrosis: stop treatment and investigate unexplained respiratory symptoms
Central venous access and ambulatory infusion pump required
Folinic acid should be omitted if fluorouracil is omitted; folinic acid may be replaced with calcium levofolinate 175mg IV
This protocol may require individual funding approval
Fluorouracil bolus must be omitted (not just reduced) for certain haematological toxicities per dose modification tables
If counts have not recovered after 2-week delay stop treatment
